input.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

payload.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

source_payload.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

pinterest_cluster_labels: light-wood Japandi dining — round table + woven pendant
floor-level tea nook — floor cushions + bamboo screens
neutral Japandi living — low sofa, sculptural wooden coffee table

pins.title: Japandi Dining Room with Neutral Decor
pins.description: A Japandi dining room with neutral décor blends Japanese simplicity and Scandinavian warmth. Soft beige, warm wood, and gentle earth tones create a calm, inviting space. Clean lines, natural materials, and minimal furnishings keep the room feeling open and uncluttered, while subtle textures like linen, ceramic, and light wood add quiet elegance and comfort.
pins.cluster_label: light-wood Japandi dining — round table + woven pendant

pins.title: Japandi nook area
pins.cluster_label: floor-level tea nook — floor cushions + bamboo screens

pins.title: Japandi Living Room: Calm Minimal Decor, Natural Wood & Indoor Plants
pins.description: Calm Japandi living room inspiration with a light neutral sofa, low wooden coffee table, soft textured rug, and plenty of indoor plants for a serene, balanced space. Minimal wall art, natural materials, and clean lines create an airy, soothing aesthetic—perfect for Japanese style interiors, minimalist living room ideas, and warm neutral home decor. Save for Japandi decor and plant-filled living room styling.
pins.cluster_label: neutral Japandi living — low sofa, sculptural wooden coffee table

cluster_summary.label: light-wood Japandi dining — round table + woven pendant
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Round oak dining table, curved mid-height upholstered chairs in linen textures, woven rattan pendant for warm upward glow; staging uses ceramic tableware, single sculptural vase and subtle wall wood slatting to elongate vertical lines.

cluster_summary.label: floor-level tea nook — floor cushions + bamboo screens
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Intimate floor seating with oversized cushioned loungers, layered natural fiber rugs and bamboo/shoji screening; low wooden tray table, ambient paper lanterns and small green plantings to create a cozy, meditative corner.

cluster_summary.label: neutral Japandi living — low sofa, sculptural wooden coffee table
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Light neutral linen sofa on low wooden base, asymmetrical sculpted oak coffee table, textured area rug and potted greenery; pared-back art and vertical wood slat accents emphasize calm proportion and organic materiality.

vibe_report.summary: A warm, calm Japandi interior that balances minimal, textured materials with family-friendly function. Warm neutral woods, soft linens, and sculpted low-profile furniture create a meditative, uncluttered atmosphere. Prioritize a compact work corner for a standing desk and a gentle crib placement in the bedroom; keep circulation clear and preserve open-plan sightlines between Nest (living), Dine (dining) and Taste (kitchen).
vibe_report.palette_rationale: Warm neutrals (beige/cream/taupe) anchored by mid-tone oak and low-contrast deep accents for depth. Palette supports morning/soft-ambient light and allows natural wood grain and woven textures to read clearly on camera.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Soft ambient base with crisp directional accents from exterior windows and a single warm woven pendant or paper lantern practical in the dining/tea nook. Maintain plausible light sources only (windows, pendant/practical fixtures).
materials: matte black metal accents (lamp stems, hardware)
textures: soft looped rugs with low pile
furniture_language: low-profile sofa and coffee table with rounded corners
round oak dining table with mid-height curved chairs
simple standing desk with integrated cable management
open shelving / tall slim bookshelf for vertical emphasis
minimal sculptural accessories (single vase, folded throws)
styling_rules: keep surfaces mostly clear — max 3 curated objects per tabletop
anchor seating with one textured rug per zone
introduce plants sparingly for scale and freshness (1–2 per main room)
use warm practicals (rattan pendant or paper lantern) above dining or tea nook
vertical slat or bookshelf accents to convey height without clutter
avoid: excessive decòr clusters or busy patterns
large tall furniture that blocks sightlines in open plan

room_guidance.headline: Open living with a calm family hub and soft work corner option
room_guidance.guidance: Frame a low linen sofa facing the glass opening to Relax; place a sculptural coffee table and textured rug at center. Add a narrow standing desk tucked beside the exterior window or bookshelf to form a compact work corner that reads as intentional but unobtrusive. Keep toy storage subtle — woven baskets under bench seating.
must_include: visible front windows / glass opening toward Relax

room_guidance.headline: Light-wood dining bay with woven pendant and calm table setting
room_guidance.guidance: Use a round oak table centered under a single woven rattan pendant. Three-panel bay windows are the primary light; stage with a single sculptural vase and minimal ceramic tableware. Keep chairs mid-height and in linen upholstery to keep proportions airy.

room_guidance.headline: Quiet bedroom with family needs: crib and compact standing desk
room_guidance.guidance: Place the bed low on the longest wall with the crib near the exterior window for soft daylight. Create a small standing desk workstation in a corner with vertical shelving above to keep floor area clear. Use soft layered bedding, a subtle rug, and one sculptural wall piece.

room_guidance.headline: Balanced, airy kitchen with clean lines opening to dining bay
room_guidance.guidance: Keep counters clutter-free; emphasize warm wood lower cabinets and matte neutral upper surfaces. Show the opening toward Dine clearly; add a small vase or ceramic bowl as a single counter focal point. Derive all light from the dining bay and task pracs only.

room_guidance.headline: Sunroom/patio as bright calm extension with floor-level tea nook option
room_guidance.guidance: Stage a low floor cushion nook or small bench with natural-fiber rug and bamboo/shoji screening. Ensure large glass doors/windows read as the primary light source and that outdoor view is neutral and consistent with time of day.
must_avoid: strong artificial interior light overpowering window light

warnings: Floor plan scale unspecified for several rooms; approximate furniture placements must respect measured Nest/Dream/Dine dimensions.
Window positions for Relax and some closets were inferred from graphic — confirm before precise sun-path staging.
Preserve exact architecture, verticals and furniture layout when generating image/video (no architectural edits).

creative_spec.overall_mood: Soft, grounded Japandi — warm neutrals, tactile wood, low-profile family-focused furnishings.
global_style_notes: Prioritize realistic light derived from visible windows and single practical pendants only.
Keep camera exposures stable; avoid over-HDR or surreal contrast.
Maintain believable human scale — include small objects (books, cup) for scale when needed.
Curate minimal kid-friendly elements (crib, woven toy baskets) rather than overt nursery themes.
creative_spec.negative_prompt: blur, distort, low quality, warped architecture, flicker, unstable camera, unrealistic lighting, muddy shadows, layout drift
